title: Secure Disposal — Asset Decommission Wipe Confirmation Before CMDB Retire

compliance.assurance_rationale: ISO/IEC 27001 A.7.14 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that ISO 27001 A.7.14 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.6.24: Secure Disposal — Asset Decommission Wipe Confirmation Before CMDB Retire.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.6.24' running on intune:device_action; snow:cmdb_ci, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=itsm sourcetype=snow:cmdb_ci operational_status=Retired earliest=-180d
| eval retire_epoch=strptime(sys_updated_on,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| join name [| search index=mdm sourcetype=intune:device_action action=wipe | stats latest(_time) as wipe_time by device_name | rename device_name as name]
| where isnull(wipe_time) OR wipe_time > retire_epoch
description: Requires wipe or crypto-erase confirmation before CMDB retirement timestamp for media disposal chain of custody.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for ISO 27001 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes itsm, mdm and sourcetypes intune:device_action, snow:cmdb_ci. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
grandmaExplanation: Requires wipe or crypto-erase confirmation before CMDB retirement timestamp for media disposal chain of custody — so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
implementation: (1) Align name keys; (2) legal hold exceptions; (3) asset disposal vendor attestation upload. Indexes required: `index=itsm`, `index=mdm`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=intune:device_action`, `sourcetype=snow:cmdb_ci`.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Intune, ServiceNow CMDB.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `intune:device_action`; `snow:cmdb_ci`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Align name keys; (2) legal hold exceptions; (3) asset disposal vendor attestation upload.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype=snow:cmdb_ci operational_status=Retired earliest=-180d
| eval retire_epoch=strptime(sys_updated_on,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| join name [| search index=mdm sourcetype=intune:device_action action=wipe | stats latest(_time) as wipe_time by device_name | rename device_name as name]
| where isnull(wipe_time) OR wipe_time > retire_epoch
```

Understanding this SPL

**Secure Disposal — Asset Decommission Wipe Confirmation Before CMDB Retire (A.7.14)** — Requires wipe or crypto-erase confirmation before CMDB retirement timestamp for media disposal chain of custody.

Documented **Data sources**: `intune:device_action`; `snow:cmdb_ci`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Intune, ServiceNow CMDB. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: itsm; **sourcetype**: snow:cmdb_ci. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=itsm, sourcetype=snow:cmdb_ci, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **retire_epoch** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(wipe_time) OR wipe_time > retire_epoch` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: device_name, name, retire_epoch, wipe_time.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with the GRC system, audit register, or framework crosswalk. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table, Timeline
knownFalsePositives: Access reviews lag during HR transitions, manager changes, or quarterly review cycles.
